title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, no zero-days
description: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 120 vulnerabilities including 29 critical RCE flaws. CVE-2026-41089 is a critical stack-based buffer overflow in Windows Netlogon granting SYSTEM privileges. No zero-days exploited in the wild — the first zero-day-free cycle since June 2024.

title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026
description: CVE-2026-20182 is an authentication bypass in Cisco SD-WAN that lets remote attackers gain admin privileges. Exploited in targeted attacks by sophisticated threat actor UAT-8616. Sixth Cisco SD-WAN zero-day exploited this year.

title: F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities in BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX
description: F5's May 2026 quarterly security notification details 51 high and medium-severity vulnerabilities impacting BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX. Administrators should prioritise patching internet-facing load balancers and application delivery controllers.

title: CVE-2026-9082: Unauthenticated RCE in Drupal Under Active Exploitation
description: CVE-2026-9082 allows unauthenticated remote code execution, privilege escalation, and information disclosure in Drupal. Security firms are already tracking attacks against thousands of websites. Drupal has issued an emergency patch.

title: Software Supply Chain Attack Targets Multiple PHP Laravel-Lang Packages
description: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a fresh software supply chain attack campaign targeting multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang. Affected packages and tags were published in rapid succession on May 22–23, 2026. Developers are advised to audit composer.lock files immediately.

title: Megalodon Campaign Pushes 5,718 Malicious Commits to GitHub Repositories in Six Hours
description: A new automated campaign called Megalodon pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. The campaign targets CI/CD pipelines by injecting malicious workflow steps. GitHub has suspended the associated accounts.

title: New Linux Malware "Showboat" Targets Post-Exploitation of Linux Systems
description: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed details of Showboat, a new Linux malware designed for post-exploitation. It spawns a remote shell, transfers files, and functions as a SOCKS5 proxy, making it well-suited for stealthy lateral movement inside enterprise environments.

title: Canadian Man Arrested for Operating KimWolf DDoS Botnet Infecting Two Million Devices
description: U.S. and Canadian authorities arrested and charged a Canadian man accused of operating the KimWolf DDoS botnet, which allegedly infected nearly two million devices worldwide. The botnet was available as a DDoS-for-hire service.

title: Palo Alto: Frontier AI Models Now Finding Majority of New CVEs in Patch Wednesday Advisories
description: Palo Alto Networks' May 2026 Defender's Guide reports that frontier AI models discovered the majority of the 26 CVEs in its latest Patch Wednesday advisories. AI is now operating at a speed and scale that fundamentally changes the vulnerability discovery landscape for both attackers and defenders.

title: Microsoft's Multi-Model Agentic Security System Tops Leading Industry Benchmark
description: Microsoft's new multi-model agentic security system achieved 96% recall on 28 MSRC cases spanning five years in clfs.sys and 100% recall on 7 MSRC cases in tcpip.sys, topping industry benchmarks for AI-driven vulnerability triage.

title: IBM Joins Project Glasswing with Anthropic to Protect Critical Software Infrastructure
description: IBM expanded its AI cybersecurity efforts by joining Project Glasswing, a security initiative with Anthropic and other technology firms. The move comes as security executives warn that frontier AI models have sharply accelerated attackers' ability to identify and exploit software vulnerabilities.

title: Cognizant Launches Secure AI Services to Help Enterprises Scale Agentic Systems
description: Cognizant announced Secure AI Services, an integrated offering designed to help enterprises secure, govern, and scale AI and agentic systems. The launch addresses a significant gap: 63% of organisations cannot enforce purpose limitations on AI agents.

title: May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 137 Vulnerabilities, No Zero-Days — First Clean Cycle in Years
description: SOCRadar's analysis of May 2026 Patch Tuesday covers 137 total vulnerabilities with 47% being elevation-of-privilege flaws. The absence of zero-days marks the first clean Patch Tuesday cycle since June 2024, giving security teams a window to catch up on backlogged patching.

title: Microsoft Ships Azure Linux 4.0 for Cloud AI Workloads
description: Microsoft announced Azure Linux 4.0 GA at Open Source Summit North America 2026. The Fedora-derived, RPM-based distribution is purpose-built for AI and cloud-native workloads on Azure, with hardened defaults and optimised kernel configurations for inference at scale.

title: Open Source Summit NA 2026: AI Infrastructure, Supply Chain Security, and Agentic Systems Take Centre Stage
description: The Linux Foundation's 2026 summit in Minneapolis showcased the convergence of AI infrastructure, agentic systems, embedded Linux, and software supply chain security. Microsoft presented Azure Linux 4.0 alongside multiple open-source AI toolchain announcements.

title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition — Krebs on Security
description: Brian Krebs covers the May 2026 Patch Tuesday, highlighting the critical Netlogon vulnerability CVE-2026-41089, the significance of a zero-day-free cycle, and advice for prioritising the 29 critical RCE patches. Microsoft Office preview-pane exploitation vectors are called out as especially urgent.

title: Gartner Identifies Top Cybersecurity Trends for 2026: AI-Native Defense, Supply Chain, and Compliance
description: Gartner's top cybersecurity trends for 2026 include AI-native threat detection, software supply chain integrity, post-quantum cryptography readiness, and the expanding regulatory compliance burden. Organisations are advised to prioritise AI governance frameworks alongside traditional controls.

title: WEF Global Cybersecurity Outlook 2026: AI-Assisted Attacks and Widening Skills Gap
description: The World Economic Forum's 2026 cybersecurity outlook highlights that AI-assisted attacks are accelerating faster than defensive capabilities. The report notes a widening global cybersecurity skills gap and calls for increased public-private collaboration on critical infrastructure protection.

title: New Infosec Products of the Week: May 22, 2026
description: Help Net Security's weekly roundup includes Trust3 AI's MCP Security standard for safeguarding enterprise agentic AI, Babel Street's Insights Investigator for AI-assisted investigations, and several new SIEM and threat-intelligence integrations announced at RSA Conference 2026.

title: Supply Chain Attacks, AI Security, and Major Breaches Define Cybersecurity This Week in May 2026
description: eSecurity Planet's weekly roundup covers the Laravel-Lang supply chain attack, the Megalodon GitHub commit-injection campaign, ongoing Drupal exploitation, and the broader trend of AI-assisted offensive security tooling lowering the bar for sophisticated attacks against enterprise targets.
